changesRequestedReviews.author: reviewer
changesRequestedReviews.body: High-level design issue — please discuss in a follow-up PR.

instructions: Cancel in-progress CI runs first: for each ID under `## In-progress runs`, run `gh run cancel <id>` before applying code fixes. If `gh` reports a run is already completed, ignore it and continue with the next ID.
Apply code fixes: read and edit each file referenced under `## Review threads` and `## Actionable comments` above.
For each bullet under `## Changes-requested reviews` above: read the review body and apply the requested changes.
Commit changed files: `git add <files> && git commit -m "<descriptive message>"`

Keep the PR title and description current: if the changes alter the PR's scope or intent, run `gh pr edit 42 --title "<new title>" --body "<new body>"` to reflect them. Skip if the existing title/body still accurately describe the PR.
Rebase and push: `git fetch origin && git rebase origin/main && git push --force-with-lease` — capture `HEAD_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)`
Run the `resolve:` command shown above, substituting "$HEAD_SHA" with the pushed commit SHA and $DISMISS_MESSAGE with a one-sentence description of what you changed.

For any large decisions or rejections you made this iteration, add or update a `## Shepherd Journal` section in the PR description (`gh pr edit 42 --body …`) summarizing each decision. For threads and comments, use the markdown link shown in its heading above; for reviews, reference the review ID. If this section already exists, append your entries under it instead of creating a duplicate heading.

CI needs time to run on the new push. Single-shot continuation: schedule one session-only follow-up task to run `npx pr-shepherd 42` to recheck once after a fresh delay between 30 seconds and 4 minutes, then end this turn. Do not sleep or rerun inline.
